exact_required_caveat: Naming caveat: this object was previously referenced as canonical_historical_research_matchup_object. That name is resolved to mlb_matchup_table_base by this package. No PIT risk — naming only.
notes: Self-audit Q8 confirms no lookahead leakage. Q13 confirms no files modified / no background tasks / no commits. Registry matches actual shape. Substitute-in-use status is a naming issue not a PIT issue.
PIT support from Stack Acceptance Memo (shift(1) verified empirically) and base object self-audit Q8 (all rolling windows are lookback-only).

exact_required_caveat: Process caveat: builder self-audit falsely claimed no background tasks. Background tasks WERE used. Substantive PIT-safety was independently verified by forensic audit. Data integrity confirmed but process attestation from builder is not trustworthy.
notes: Substantive PIT is confirmed (no future data in features) but process non-pristine. This is the highest-risk family in the package. Risk is contained because independent verification was performed.

exact_required_caveat: SC averaging caveat: rolling windows use available-only averaging. A last_3 window with 1 unenriched start produces a value from 2 observations indistinguishable from a fully observed window. This is a data-quality caveat not a PIT-safety caveat — no future data is used.
notes: All rolling windows are lookback-only (Stack Acceptance Memo + base object Q8). The averaging caveat affects data quality/precision not temporal integrity.
Per-start realized stats. Box-score data is post-game by definition. SC enrichment is same-game. No future data.
Per-batter-per-game SC quality. Same-game data. No future leakage. Feeds lineup_state aggregation which applies shift(1).
Game-level umpire assignment. Umpire identity and ratings are known pre-game. No future data.
Prior-season umpire ratings. By definition uses only completed prior seasons. No future data.

exact_required_caveat: Documentation-only caveat: no standalone data artifact exists. Fix applied directly to config.py. Repair docs record what changed but cannot be independently verified from the package alone without inspecting config.py.
notes: PIT risk is low because the repair affected static umpire rating constants not rolling/temporal features. But signoff support is weak because there is no data artifact.
Game-level weather. Observed conditions. No future data.
Game-level park context. Static park factors. No future data.
Static mapping table. No temporal component. No PIT concern.
Documentation artifact. Records the forensic acceptance basis for the 5 substrates.
Game-level spine. actual_total is a post-game outcome used as the target variable. No future leakage — outcomes are observed results.

exact_required_caveat: Same caveat as mlb_bullpen_substrate: builder self-audit was false about background tasks. This is the production copy of that same substrate. Substantive PIT verified independently.
notes: Same underlying data as mlb_bullpen_substrate. Inherits its process caveat.
